reviews.review_title: Cool game but unfinished with no ending!
reviews.review_description: The game wasn’t bad. I enjoyed the graphics. The gameplay reminded me a little bit of Metro awakening and RE￼ I thought the weapon upgrade system was cool. The story had potential, but it didn’t have a ending, not even credits after you complete the last mission. The screen goes black and it pops back to the main menu. With no understanding of what happened Smh￼. Drop a dlc or something because right now I feel like I wasted my time on this

After 7 patches, you would think this game would be so much better, just picked it up on 07/05/26  and boy are there still issues , more than once the load your gun screen will not disappear from from view, now this should have the first thing to fix, lots of jank still, as I never played the earlier version I’m not sure how much has improved, I’m on the fence if I will continue or refund,  I want to love it but it’s just not consistently in a good place.

*update, yes in places it’s a bit janky but I can see the improvements the devs have made, one or two issues still exist but overall it runs well now.

Ok. So I was hyped for this game then realised it had lots of issues at launch so I let it marinate a bit with updates before I tried it.
There are still some glaring issues but it is mostly playable!

Now, I love the idea, I love the storyline, the setting. And I love the world created around this Lovecraftian concept! The atmosphere is great, the visuals work well, and there is a lot put in to the surroundings! Interesting ideas for levels! The voice acting is mostly at a decent standard, the script a bit sloppy but it works!
There's a cool upgrade system and the weapons are solid in their own way. The story is very Resident Evil meets Dead Space. And I'm here for it. 
The puzzles aren't very intuitive, but they're also not too complex.
There's some cool enemies, and the variety kicks in around chapter 3, but when you fight them they fall a bit flat and the impact is lacking. But it can still be scary and it's cool taking out the limbs and such.
You should give this a try, and i love what they have tried to do. We do need to support more VR games like this because this is what the platform needs instead of Gorilla Tag type slop and AI heavy chaos stuff.
And this is made by a small team and I think a first foray into VR. But that being said, some of the glitches are glaring and they do need to do better quality testing before releasing. 
Items can glitch out, go flying everywhere, the machete is so glitchy and when you try to break locks with it you have to really line it up so awkwardly well otherwise it phases through...but it is mostly workable and enjoyable.
Though 2 massive issues for me, the second one actually worrying. In chapter 3 it glitched me into the floor, so I was in a perpetual crouch. I tried reloading and found that I was in that weird crouch even in earlier saves, where I was actually halfway through the floor. I kept reloading the save point until it finally decided to put me back standing again. So I suggest to anyone else with similar trouble, try to do that to rectify. 
Now the second issue, and very angering...some of the sound is dangerously loud in the headset. I didn't realise until later that day I had a ringing in my ear and lost the hearing in that ear for a bit. I wasn't sure if it was the game, so I went back and turned the sound down in the game and the headset. Yet some of these sound effects (rumblings) are so intrusive and pierce through your eardrum. This is incredibly dangerous and I hope that the devs do read this and immediately rectify it. So anyone playing should watch out for this. I still want people to get the game, to support it and enjoy it. But that sound thing needs to be changed right now! I want VR to succeed! I want games like this! I want the devs to win! I want to understand struggles of making the games, but for heaven's sakes get some quality testers!

I will get the good out of the way. It got resident evil gameplay vibes all over it. Some of the puzzles are ok

Negatives: 
- game runs like garbage. Huge dips in frame rates
- sluggish controls
- jank sound effects, lips dont match voices, delayed sounds on actions
- graphics have moments of excellence but jagged edges and weird pop in make it hard to enjoy
- enemy ai is painfully stupid, embarrassingly bad, they get stuck, teleport all over the place will stop chasing you if you stand in spots
- this game should of stayed in the oven longer

I get so disappointed when I pre-order a game to see the "unreal engine" banner in the title. Almost all games made from this engine on quest run and look terrible. 

The pace of this game is jarring, there is no time to breath and take in your surroundings. Blaring horror music that doesnt match what your doing. 

I can keep going, im going to finish it, but only bc im starving for vr games. The best games came out 7 to 9 years ago. All that's left is well intentioned jank fests and shovelware its so disappointing that the quest store just allows buggy jank to get on their platform. 

Also I dont know how so many 5 star reviews came out on launch day. Especially ones saying its scary, its not. Its a headache simulatior. Sad thing is it was close to being decent but I think the choice to use unreal instead of fmod is what kills this. 

I wish the quest store would let you look at games released oldest to newest. Bc the games that have come out in the last year have been absolutely abysmal compared to what used to come out when I first got my quest 2. Wtf? I love vr and im watching it die in real time. So disappointing

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your detailed feedback on the game, and hear your frustration around the many difficulties you ran in to. We are working hard to address all the issues you've mentioned, especially for the Quest 2.

We'd love to hear more about the problems you've ran in to. If you're interested in sharing more, please send us details to contact_dreadmeridian@levelinfinite.com or reach out to us on our Discord https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_description: Holy moly is this game INTENSE! A constant presence of feeling dread like Metro awakening, and the horrors being chased by a psychopath and monsters like resident evil. this game helps fill the void since resident evil 4 and Metro Awakening and is an absolute TERRIFYING experience. Absolutely gory, this isn’t for the light hearted! Try to escape from a murdering lunatic and monsters at your peril! Highly recommend!

developer_response.body: Thanks AwkwardTwinkie! Everyone here on the team really appreciate your glowing review! We worked hard to bring this horror atmosphere to life and are excited that players are really feeling it.

reviews.review_description:
I've been playing VR for many years and been a gamer for 40 years so when I say this is underrated I'm saying it after playing thousands of games.

Great story great level design great atmosphere.  Some imperfections but there's not a lot of original story driven action horrors like this.

I'm engrossed and have killed two batteries so far.

The studio behind this should take credit for what they achieved so far 

Feels like a cross between the thing, cthulu and Resident Evil.

Well done guys can't wait to see what's next.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the amazing review!

reviews.review_description:
I read about this lovecraft inspired horror game before I got my quest 3 and it was one I was keen to buy straight away. Maybe I should I wait till I finish it before reviewing, but I might update it when that time comes. So far I have exited the forest and got passed those brutal dogs. I'm enjoying the story so far and using notes and audio cells to learn more is fun and interactive. There are moments in the story where I can see the Lovecraft theme appearing and it keeps me intrigued. I just found a note referring to the shadow of some huge monster! Love it so far. The Arctic theme is works great with the horror feel, so the environments are pretty cool. Stepping onto the deck for the first time was awesome. I've just got a new weapon which along with the pistol looks good and works well. The knife seems to along work with cutting objects but would be too hard to use against the brutal monsters. I'm liking the main characters voice acting when pointing out things in the environment. The beginning of the game was shocking and showed me just how cool a horror game this is going to be. And the Chainsaw was cool to use. The fight wasn't as dramatic as it tried to be though. Would be better if the dead monsters didn't disappear but that's no real issue. I haven't come across many puzzles yet but the first couple were quite interesting. 
The graphics aren't the best and I the only bug or problem I've had was in the house after getting killed. The game brought me back just outside the building, where I couldnt get back in the house. And reloading kept bringing me back there. I was worried I would have to start the game again but I just closed the game and loaded my game and it put me back in the building so that fixed it. Though not a heavily inspired lovecraft game, just yet anyway, I can still see this as being close to another Dark Corners of the Earth type of game. Can't wait to get back into it and see what happens. But as it is, I'm already wanting a sequel.

I'm starting at 4 stars due to its potential. Definitely striving for that AAA on a budget feel. The presentation is good and if its close to the resident evil inspiration vr needs more of it. 

The work it needs
*Animations are unpolished. Sliding down the hill in the opening felt like floating. And sometimes I skip into place.
*Item interactions sometimes puts your hands in odd positions
*Can't see ammo belt and don't need a large visual every time you pick some up. (Same for most vision obscuring visuals)
*I'm at the first lever puzzle and I keep grabbing my knife.
*if health is an injectable. Animate the injection.

If they're still working on this then it could be 5 stars. If not and these pile up could be much lower. They aren't charging a lot so I'll give them time to cook.

Lost a star. I got the sub machine gun put it won't let me grab it from my shoulder and the prompt won't go away even in the menu. Definitly needed to be in the oven longer.

Lost another star. Ladders feel like moving in mud. Errors piling up. Im at the blind creature and it doesnt stay distracted long enough to turn the crank and locks you into place making instant death unavoidable.

Lost its last star for an abrupt crappy ending that explained nothing.

These 5-star reviews are either paid or tied to the studio.  This game was not ready for release.  It is plagued with a host of issues that I cant even list them all.  Broken mechanics, assets, visual glitches, pop in/out, and so many more. Game breaking issues like a certain place in the game you get stuck in the floor.  Each and every time you restart the save, game, and/or headset. Can't finish.

I understand this is a very small team/guy making this game. This is beyond indie jank.  It just needs more time and polish.  I hate to tear apart anyone's creation.  I had hype for this game. It's everything I want in a vr game, and there are great bones here, it just needs to be finished.

I hope you finish this. It's not a bad game, its a bad build

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback! We're working hard on improving the game based on everyone's feedback. 

If you have more details you wish to provide on your issues, so we can address them more directly, please reach out to us via Discord at https://discord.gg/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_description: I've been seeing this on tiktok, saw the graphics and i gave it some slack due to it being a game on a standalone headset and such, and i thought that it would be a great scary game, i was then hit with choppy framerate (quest 3s), sketchy movement, clumsy inventory system, no difficulty options, delayed sound effects/chase ost, Genuinely out of place puzzles, invasive UI elements (Ex: picking up a key item and it taking up half your screen), flying ragdolls, choppy gunplay and terrible cutscenes (examples being: the slope jumpscare, your hands fly far away from you and breaks immersion and the wake up scene, and possibly many more. and here i thought the reviews were just hating 7yr olds but man, seriously i'm disappointed i bought this.

This game has such potential, but the games movement is terrible often walking or.sliding passed where intended and the game constantly crashes with the gun being locked out of your hand. I even left it for a month or so to let the devs work on it. No dice and a real bummer.

So I am in the section in the mine right now.  So far the game has lived up to the “dread” in its name. Let’s start with the good stuff.  1. Creepy Atmosphere love it. 2 new patch has fixed some launch issues “Some Issues”. 3. Story so far is not too bad.  Now some bad stuff. 1. Then Knife is 100% useless it takes 2-3 swings at a lock for it just to register that I am trying to break a lock. Knife is of no use in combat as it does so little damage I might as well being hitting monsters with a pillow. 2. Do not expect to ever have any bullets EVER. it takes so many bullets to kill one bad guy (even when hitting weak spots) that your supplies will be empty after maybe 2 fights. Ridiculous!!!  I know this is supposed to be survival horror but come on. Memoreum did this SOOOOOO MUCH BETTER.  Why can’ti get out my sub machine gun with out going into my inventory now?…this was working fine I’d reach over my shoulder and grab it now nothing! WTF? I want to like this game but it just has so much wrong right now.

Update. So the new update is out. And guess what nothing is any better. Knife is still 100 percent useless. Takes about 15-20 strikes to knock down one of those flying things in the mine. NOT kill JUST Knock them down once. Still NO BULLETS. why even have any guns in the game if i NEVER have ammo?  I shot one guy in the head (weak spot) 5 times with a shot gun and it did nothing. Give me some way to defend myself.  Oh yeah I STILL can’t get my large weapons out without having to go into my inventory. I know some will say limited ammo is the challenge. But if I hit the guy 5 times with a freaking shotgun SOMETHING should happen.

I play tested this game before release,  3 separate play test for 1 week at a  time. If u liked lies beneath,  re4, metro awaking like I did, u will love this game. Super freaky creepy chilling high action adventure. Rarely we get games of this level with superb grahics and design. Its a gem.

developer_response.body: Hi Greg, we really appreciate your positive review, and your participation in our previous playtests. It makes a huge difference to us on the dev team.

reviews.review_title: A Masterclass in VR Survival Horror!
reviews.review_description:
Just to clarify this is a completely impartial review and I came into this game without any preconceptions deliberately. That said, I can confidently say this is one of the best VR horror games I’ve played in a long time.

The level design is immaculate and thoughtfully crafted, and the visuals are absolutely phenomenal. I downloaded it seven hours ago and haven’t taken a single break since. I went in completely blind, no reviews, no gameplay footage, barely even knew the premise and I’m so glad I did. What an incredible surprise.

I’ve just beaten one of the early bosses, a massive Lovecraftian slime creature, and I don’t think I’ve ever felt that tense in VR before. This is exactly what survival horror should feel like in virtual reality; immersive, nerve-shredding, and unforgettable.

I’m not sure how far through the game I am yet, but I already don’t want it to end. It’s one of those rare titles that you think about all day at work, just counting the hours until you can get back home and strap the headset on again. A truly special experience.

As a side note, I’ve seen a few reviews after putting some time into the game and I have to say I disagree with some of them. I’ve experienced no bugs or glitches so far. I’ve also seen comments about people struggling to even fire the pistol, but if you take a bit of time to learn the mechanics and get used to how it works, it functions perfectly fine. Like some VR games, there’s a small learning curve, once it clicks, it feels great!

Bravo to the team behind this, you’ve created something exceptional.

reviews.review_title: The game is the definition of jank
reviews.review_description: i really wanna like this game and i played it on quest two and it just ran like absolute garbage even with side quest on to boost the graphics. so i finally got a metaquest three thinking maybe the extra power would help and it does not the game just is terribly optimized after being out for month it still isn't where it needs to be it still need a year to cook

I normally never review vr games but this? This is absolutely amazing. Despite it being only day one of this game being out, its phenomenal. Like any other new release it has some bugs and some glitches but they are well easy to get past. The atmosphere is scary and theres a-lot to look at. Its the type of game thats in your face survival horror like Resident Evil ( if you tell me this was not inspired by RE, i wont believe you) I played this game till my battery’s were almost dead and i wanted to keep playing. I cant wait to see what more lies in store for me. Keep up the awesome work! 😊

I’m currently on the first chapter and I have thoroughly enjoyed my experience so far. Today, I was able to complete the second half of the first puzzle after the most recent patch to the game was implemented. The Devs are still tending to this game after release with patches which is really nice!

I pre-ordered this game and had hope of it being as good as Paradise Hotel, but it’s far below that standard. The game runs at poor frame rates and the animations on the NPC’s are bad even by VR’s standards. To escape the first enemy you duck under a table and fall into a hole but my character was stuck on the table and I couldn’t progress. Maybe a stability patch can fix these problems but in its current state I would want a refund. 
Giving an extra star because the premise/concept are great and I want this game to improve.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the honest feedback! We appreciate your patience with any issues you are running in to and are working diligently to improve the game. 

We would love for you to share more details about the what problems have occured for you. Feel free to reach out to us at contact_dreadmeridian@levelinfinite.com or our Discord https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR with more info.

reviews.review_description:
Imagine watching a horror movie and just as you start to get immersed and creeped out someone from the film crew strolls through the background carrying a prop while yakking on a cell phone. The scene is ruined and you're taken right out of the movie.

This is what playing a janky, glitchy VR horror game like Dread Meridian is like.  It's a super frustrating experience.

My initial review was all griping about the muddy Quest 2 graphics and wishing it took full advantage of the Quest 3.

The graphics are the least of this game's problems.

After playing it for an hour or so I got a glimpse of its potential.  It could be a truly scary horror game, something that doesn't come along often enough for me.

But every time I found myself becoming immersed and unnerved in the atmosphere the horror was short lived because something janky or glitchy would come along and ruin it.

This game is absolutely riddled with technical issues. It's very, very far away from being ready for sale, so I'm going to request a refund and hope that they'll finish it someday so I can buy it again and really enjoy it.

reviews.review_title: WAY better than early reviews suggest
reviews.review_description: I held off on this one after trying the demo on Steam, which was a mess. Early reviews were bad. However, the trailer looked so good I kept going back to it, hoping the updates would help the game live up to it. Happy to say that despite some forgivable indie jank, Dread Meridian is one of the best horror experiences on Quest. I loved the atmosphere, graphics and storytelling. Sure enemies will occasionally clip through walls, freeze in place, or die with hilarious rag doll physics, but for me it’s all part of the charm once you meet the game on its own level. Strongly recommend!

I wish people would wait for patches before giving negative reviews. Yes the game has some issues, and they will be fixed. People dont realize we need to support small teams, especially in the current VR environment in 2026. They did a great job, the scenery was great on the Quest. Yes there is some jank that will be taken care of.  I think people expect $50 AAA experience from a $19 indie game….or they want FREE to play.  Please wait on your ratings for patches BEFORE you make your reviews.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the positive feedback! We are working hard to address the issues that players are experiencing and hope to make everyone's gameplay as smooth as possible!

reviews.review_title: I really love this, needs a little work
reviews.review_description:
I’ll start with the good.

- The atmosphere and exploration are phenomenal. 
- Plenty of hidden areas and rewards for exploring off the beaten path. 
- Weapon upgrades are fun and look very cool. 
- Moment to moment gameplay and the story development are brilliant (so far anyway, I’m a few hours into the second major area). 
- Graphics and world design look phenomenal on Q3 (with optimiser) and I can say I never personally had any major visual issues.

And the not so good, needs a few QOL tweaks: 

- Manual save slots would make a huge difference especially as other users have reported performance issues. 
- The “grab” area for weapons isn’t clear or rather too large, I find myself grabbing the gun/knife when trying to interact with the environment. Changing holster placement doesn’t seem to work.
- The text pop ups aren’t quite right, can’t put my finger on it but I find my focus blurring due to the placement of the text 

Besides that I’ve had an absolute blast with this game and it nails the RE vibe while managing to be its own thing. Would be five stars easily if the kinks get ironed out!

EDIT: Finished the game and very disappointed. Chapters 1-3 are a blast to play, then the last two start building the story before it all just…..ends.

Well that's the game completed a little while ago, pointless waiting for any more improvement updates now, may have a blast in the future again when its improved 

But for now I will say all in all, great story, great graphics on Q3 for just about everything and monsters were  really good, and despite the improvements suggestions I am ||disappointed in the game length, I take my words back because of the length, it is worth only £13, and the ending so disappointed in that, never thought it would be over just like that :frowning2:, could have been a lot more to this game but nope, another great horror vr game  and it was great as the music, sounds/noises pretty freaked me out at times and a lot of effort put in graphics wise but let down mainly by the length, I do hope they can DLC it or something, another game also where you get all weapon upgrades finished near the end so no real using them a lot at all which I hate that with games, hence I always recommend a new game plus added for games like this, so you get to use everything upgraded, I only got use the fully upgraded shot gun once or twice and that was on the final boss with the 4 shells I only had left as again very little ammo 

Never mind, decent game, great gore and horror but the length and other things mentioned previously let is down, please extend the game like DLC, as with the ending it could be possible :thumbsup:||

It maybe, maybe worth 3 and a half stars But you can't so sorry it gets 3 stars because of length and the disappointing ending, improvements can be done with other things in the game but the main thing it could have been a lot longer

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write up such detailed feedback. We are working hard on improving the game, and hope you can revisit it later on and enjoy it even more!

reviews.review_description: I’m trying so hard to like this game but it’s just not happening. For a game released in 2026, it doesn’t even compare to games that have been released YEARS before, like RE4. The fact they had to bust out 6 patches in a week just shows what kind of state the game was in. It’s still playable and some parts are fun, but there is SO much room for improvement.

developer_response.body: We've been working hard to address all the concerns raised upon launch, and hope you can give Dread Meridian another shot!

reviews.review_description: Not sure why everyone is bagging on this game.  Gameplay is definitely RE inspired, but with a fun and original story.  Runs great and looks good on quest 3.  Price is right too

I bought this game as a preorder because it was a co op my sister and I could play and to my surprise when my sister tried to buy the game she said it is no longer a co op game I’m sure it’s a good single player but bought the game under fake advertisement

developer_response.body: Our sincerest apologies for the misunderstanding. We do still have a 2v2 multiplayer mode available if that would interest either of you.

reviews.review_description:
So far I’m loving it. Quest needed this game 🫵🤘🔥 Not sure about the negative reviews but the game works fine and is fun af for me. Updates smoothed it out even more 🤜🤛
Wait it just ended. That’s it?

reviews.review_title: I loved this game until I met the giant
reviews.review_description:
I loved this game until I met the giant blind monster in chapter 4. Up to then, it was not too easy, not too difficult, just right, and I thoroughly enjoyed every second, as in, best game I've ever played, omg never been so scared etc. Then I met this blind giant on chapter 4 and even chat GPT couldn't help me with a strategic successful approach. I died about twenty times and no solution in sight. Chat gpt concluded that the last patch made this particular section of the game quite unwinnable for most, and advised to wait for the next patch in order to continue the game. I could only find one full gameplay on YouTube with chapter 4 in it, and that was before the latest patch, so I couldn't follow the approach there with any success. Regardless, this game is crazy good, worth the ticket straightaway from the start, I actually cannot believe what I paid and what I got, it felt like half life alyx or resident evil, so I totally recommend this game, specially that it is new so it's going to get even better in the next few weeks and months
 👏💪🔫👽👍🙏

UPDATE: OK a few hours later I attempt again to go past the giant blind monster.... this time the monster was a lot more inactive and it was kinda easy to try get to the next place.... Only to find it there again 😫😩🫣  anyway I don't know what happened in the previous  attempts, first too difficult, then too easy,  perhaps the variability in the monster's behaviour goes too far into extremes. Now I'm trapped with this heinous creature in another floor full of steaming pipes and not knowing where you go. Oh boy, hate it lol 😆😀

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that you've had difficulties with this section! If you need any help, feel free to reach out to us on Discord https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_title: Please fix it!!! Could be a gem
reviews.review_description:
Atmosphere is great, grafix is great.
Combat and Weapon mechanic arent good.
(Wepons look great)
When i load a save game i either stuck in a bed for example or fall trough the ground, so i have to load a way older savegame and my progress is gone. Enemy AI sucks! They just stand around till you walk close to them. Some glitch trough walls, doors or get stuck in Doors.
Please put some work in it, it could be such a great game

developer_response.body: Thanks for the honest feedback! We're working hard on improving the game based on everyone's feedback. If you have more details you wish to provide on your issues, please reach out to us via Discord at https://discord.gg/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_description:
I recently purchased Dread Meridian on my Quest 3s, I was so hyped for this game but man the fall is hard...

The game has great potential but is full of bugs that constantly get you out of the experience

The dev team seems to be actively publishing patches but as of patch .7 I still experience a lot of bugs and the reporting mail seems to be inaccessible as for the Discord channel for feedbacks were I am not allowed to post, so if any dev reads this, here is my share of encountered issues (only chapter 1) :

- files reading mechanic with a/b for scrolling is clumsy

- when taking the wood elevator if you keep your grip on the hook instead of just releasing it then the physic is weird and you go up/down by moving your hand

- sounds are sometimes a little out of sync, doors squeak a little too much

- the slide in the snow just after the deer and wolfs is kinda weird, seems like you're jumping up in the air and not sliding

- breaking locks with the knife is a headache, first I thought I didn't get the right item as my attempts did do nothing until shaking the knife like a maniac against the lock

- gun's mechanic is buggy, first time I got it I tried reloading it by habit and it dropped the charger IN the ground and I couldnt get it back even when reloading the checkpoint, I needed to redo the whole chapter, also general instructions about using it are kinda confusing (when to load chamber, shells dropped for nothing etc...)

- for brewing the potion of true sight I am kinda sure the name of plants are mixed up when your character says it out loud on first pouring

- in inventory, when you switch between items and files panels, the lighter remains visible (whereas in files)

I didn't get very far in the game as there were so many bugs/quirks already, I think the game has potential but I prefer to wait for bug fixes to really enjoy it hopefully

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback. We're still working hard to improve the game with frequent updates. You can reach out to our #support channel to share your feedback and we'd be glad to help you out.

reviews.review_description:
This game has everything it needs to be great but right now, it desperately needs patches.
I’m usually pretty forgiving when it comes to bugs and glitches, but with this game it’s hard to look past them. The constant crashing makes it really difficult to enjoy what’s otherwise a very promising experience.That said, the potential here is huge. If the developers can fix the technical issues, this could easily become one of the best horror games on Quest. You can tell the devs genuinely care about what they’re making, and that’s why I still have faith they’ll continue working on it until it reaches a much more playable state.
As of now, I’m giving it 2 stars, because I honestly don’t think anyone should play it in its current condition. I fully plan to update this review once the game gets the fixes it needs.

I want to give this game 5 stars so badly.
Please don’t let me down.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback! We're working hard on improving the game based on everyone's feedback. 

If you have more details you wish to provide on your issues, so we can address them more directly, please reach out to us via Discord at https://discord.gg/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_description:
So I bought the game thinking. “Hey this looks fun”
Oh how wrong I was. After beating the first two chapters it was pretty fun at that point. Then after the chapter 4 which I thought was chapter 3 and also the final boss WHICH i still thought was chapter 3. Then I saw what the game hyped up the pedi stool thing I placed the weird thing in it then after the cutscene I said to my self “alright time for chapter 4”. Nope the game just ends. And now I’m disappointed with the game because it has potential to be good but it decided to end to short for its own good and I said to myself “hey this game could use an extra 2 to 1 hour and a half” then I would give it a better review but for now the game just i disappointed me.

Purchased the game a couple days ago. This is my honest interpretation. 

1.I feel it's not utilizing the full capability of the quest 3. The cut scenes are well executed and look fine. The problem I come across is during some of the action I feel it's performing as if it's running on a quest 2

2) Very jankey on certain parts. For instance, I try breaking the locks with my knife. Many times it goes right through the chains. I have to repeat the action multiple times. Sometimes I'll be picking up a note and my hand goes right through the table. Other times my hands will be floating away from my body. It doesn't happen all the time but is definitely noticeable throughout. Also, at times the creatures will literally have half their body through a door swinging at you while the door is shut? That happened on a couple occasions as well.

3) Now, for some positives. I thought the creatures were unique. They did a good job on the details. There are definitely a few jump scares that get your attention. Nicely done. The story is decent and keeps you wanting to progress further. The puzzles have a good balance. Not overly complicated but not super easy. 

All in all I'm a bit disappointed. I say that because I see the potential in this game. I feel like it needed a bit more time to work out all the kinks. It's a shame because there are some really great moments in the game where it really shines. Unfortunately, they are far and in between. Perhaps down the line they will make improvements.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great feedback! We're working on addressing all these issues, and hope you can give it a second look soon.

reviews.review_description: Initially, I fell in love with this game.. the graphics, maneuverability, scares, etc. but once I got to the room with the symbol on the floor .. the game froze and wouldn’t allow me to proceed to the next level. Despite, carrying out all necessary requirements. ChatGPT provided me walkthrough and eventually said I’d need to reinstall and hope glitch has been resolved. This sucks because I just purchased this game and was already denied a refund . Beware gamers!!! I paid 20$ bucks for a game, I’ll never play again- didn’t even get too far in the game either. Complete waste of money!

developer_response.body: We would love to help you address the issue in our Discord. Please reach out to us in the #feedback channel https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_title: Perfect hrror wth great production value
reviews.review_description: Don't listen to the bad reviews posted last week - there were three patches already provided by the developers and it's not even a week after the release. It is already a title that I can honestly recommend. It's a fully fledged VR game with a proper length, graphics and mechanics. Still hope the developers will not stop polishing a game.

I got pinned to table with that guy chasing me. I died shooting first monster escaping the house, a stupid balloon instruction wouldn’t go away, the gun is incredible hard to aim. The first puzzle is very tough unless you luck in it. Game has potential but needs a lot of patches.

developer_response.body: We apologize for the less-than-ideal experience you had, and we appreciate your recognition of our game's potential. Since launch, we've received a lot of feedback from players and are rapidly fixing bugs in the product. We will address the issues you mentioned in an upcoming patch as soon as possible. Regarding the puzzle elements, we will adjust them accordingly. If possible, please join our Discord server; we will closely follow up on your issues there. Your game feedback is very valuable to us.

reviews.review_description:
I previously gave the game 3 stars which I now think was unfair. I did enjoy playing it. It was a decent length and I did get the crap scared out of me a few times. The beginning with the doctor was very creepy. Like I said, I had no bugs or glitches that made me not want to play. Maybe a couple frozen spots. I just carried on. I was playing on quest 3s if that makes a difference. The graphics were great. It looked very nice. 
It was definitely worth it, I enjoyed it. I'm glad I bought the game. 
I really don't know whats fair when rating games. Do you give it low stars because it's not for you. Low stars because of bugs and glitches. Or low stars for bad mechanics and horrible story? I think 3 or below should be reserved for games that are unplayable and bad mechanics and scenery.  If you can put one of these games together and you like it enough to play through, it deserves at least 4 stars. I can't imagine trying to make one of these games. 
It wasn't my favorite game, but it is definitely one of the better games.

So here is my honest review of this game, the graphics were just ok and the story line was somewhat good although there were a few parts I felt lost in the store, at the end the game just seems to end suddenly leavening you feeling like your going to the next part of the game, like litterly your playing it then the game just....    ENDS! And I am like thinking, what? That was the end of the game?

developer_response.body: We appreciate the honest feedback. We're working hard on improving the game based on great feedback like yours.

reviews.review_title: Very GOOD!! 😱😱😱 But some issues now
reviews.review_description: Update : Thanks for the advice on loading the gun. Maybe my fault for not knowing!!, Also if you try to shoot the first mutant before he gets up, he freezes though you can still move around. Also the bullets seem to be hitting the target but there's no damage and you die. Definitely needs to be fixed! This IS STILL very good and the atmosphere is strong in this one. I've only just got past some of it, and I get some Ghost Town vibes except far more eerie and creepy. I don't think I've gotten to any of the extremely scary stuff yet but looking forward to it. I'm a big fan of Lovecraft so really interested in seeing where this will take me..

developer_response.body: We appreciate your detailed feedback! In order to reload your gun, you can grab a clip from your chest area directly to load into the gun, then slide the rack to be able to shoot. No need to grab bullets from the backpack. We are also looking into the AI issues.

reviews.review_title: We got absolute peak before GTA 6
reviews.review_description: I have pre-ordered this game on january 2nd, And from that day have waited for the announcement of its realese. And when it came out finally....my god.. greatest game i have played.. everything was good in it!! The Story,the gameplay And heck! Even the textures And graphics! So i gotta say..it was Worth waiting

How does a projectile vomit track your character and the knife attacks are buggy everything is buggy how have you people not gotten sued yet it can be a good game but this wasn't even playtestee nor seen a writing room. FYI its not a pvp mode its a story mode problem also can't even get past the 3rd boss in the mines cause it is so sh*t

developer_response.body: Thank you for the honest feedback on the game. As for the knife attacks issue, we are constantly working to improve the game. If you are having any issues with completing certain sections of the game, please reach out to us in the Discord (https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR). As for the MP mode, we hope you can understand that we are still iterating on the gameplay.

reviews.review_title: I would love to love this...
reviews.review_description: I will start by saying I was hyped for this and I even pre-ordered it. There is a very good game here, somewhere buried beneath the jank. Speaking of jank,  where to start...the game is almost unplayable in its current state. Half the time I am unable to use health syringes,  because the game won't let my hand close enough to my body to inject them, if that makes sense. Also, reloading your guns is a pain in this game, and often doesn't work at all when you need it to. Also, may just be my experience,  but the screen literally shakes when I'm standing still. Please devs,  please address these things. I think you have a very good product here, and although I am not looking to refund it, I will not be continuing my playthrough until more patches are released. I don't wish to sully what I believe is a fantastic game, just waiting to be freed from its bonds of controller-breaking frustration.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the honest feedback! 

We are working hard to address the issues that players are experiencing. Feel free to join our Discord (https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR) and share more details in our #feedback channel.

reviews.review_description: I played this game and it horrified me, the gore, the acting, and physics feel real! The gun feels like I’m actually using one! It’s just the puzzles are a bit tricky (I’m stuck on the one with the symbols on the door) but non the less the puzzles are ones you can find out on your own! I recommend this game to anyone wanting to play a good vr horror, it feels like alien and silent hill combined! 10/10 best vr game.
Yes this game is a bit buggy but it's absolutely fantastic. Love the monsters and excellent level design. Closest your gonna get to the thing being released in vr. Good price also👍

I love this game so far. Graphics are great, gameplay has so issues though. Like when controlling my character. Left stick needs to strafe better than the crappy speed that is allowed. If I had an option I would seriously crank the sensitivity up for this. As at this point it bugs me so much, I hardly want to play it. I can physically move my body faster, but can’t walk sideways in the game. Moves like an old crippled woman. Please fix this. I feel this should have been something, that if properly tested, someone should have said something.

I have not played a ton, but this game is fantastic, it’s incredible for its price, (26$ can) it’s scary, the gunplay is cool, and it’s probably made me the most scared I have been in a while, this game is awesome, keep it up.

reviews.review_title: Big Dreams in a Tiny Package
reviews.review_description:
I love the worldview and atmosphere created by this game—its visuals are even more exquisite and delicate than I had imagined. Despite its small file size, which made me think I’d finish it quickly, it actually took me quite a while to complete. Each level offers a unique gameplay style and experience, and I felt a bit left wanting more when it ended.
There are some minor bugs in the game, such as items occasionally falling into places where I can’t reach them, and my dagger dealing way too much damage. I believe they’ll fix these small issues, and I’ll definitely look forward to its sequel.

I was a bit nervous to buy this game after the play test when the game looked fun but nothing worked. Luckily there was a big improvement. I liked the story and the environment. Mechanics could be improved. What’s with the super slow motion climbing?!  It’s terrible and makes no sense. Enemies take a lot of shots to bring down, so even tho you find ammo pretty regularly, it’s also a bit scarce and you may run out. If i tried using the knife,  i think it took 50-100 stabs to kill and enemy, not even exaggerating   Was useless. Was a bit of a pain to unload and reload when it counted, and ammo seemed to disappear from my inventory. I couldn’t figure out how to load the shotgun until the end when it was never needed. It was a bit shorter than I was expecting but for the price was reasonable. At first it feels like you walk quickly. By the end of the game it feels like running is barely faster than walking. I swear the speed changed. A decent # of shots that were hits didn’t register and there were some similar glitches at the end during the final boss battle. There is still some jitteriness and walking thru the doorways and even just across terrain was difficult at times.

The story line is good although the ending is disappointing it just stops out of nowhere.

The getting bullets from chest needs to be the number 1 improvement im grabbing chest stomach legs they just wont grab then randomly appear in your hand not great when you keep dieing cause you cant reload your gun. 

Graphics could be better more specific in the cavey areas its alot of pixilated if improvements are worked on it could be a great game.

developer_response.body: We appreciate the specific feedback. We are constantly working to improve the experience. If you have any more feedback details, feel free to share it with us on Discord https://discord.com/invite/fNbj9BVmFR

reviews.review_title: It feels like a resident evil village
reviews.review_description: For playing the game for a bit it feels so similar to resident evil village this is really fun. I highly recommend it if you like resident evil village it’s so much fun. The gameplay the puzzles are a bit confusing. Even the gun design looks so good honestly I think this is like the best game I ever played so far. ￼

reviews.review_title: Initial reaction, maybe too soon to tell
reviews.review_description:
I’m early in but struggling with moving around in this game-I prefer smooth and when I choose to go straight, I “pull to the left”. It’s the oddest thing and I don’t experience this is any other game.
I think I’m gonna wait a while before trying this game and let the developers fix the bugs .

reviews.review_description: Despite a few bugs, I really enjoyed the game. Hopefully the Devs will keep working on it but thoroughly recommended. Ending definitely leaves you wanting more. Hopefully game will be added to in future

I like the athmosphere and the setting, but the bugs destroy these completely. For an Example. At the very beginning, when you get a gun, you stumble appon your first enemy. When you die and respawn, your gun is gone and you have absolutly no Option to defend yourselfe. Very frustrating.

Please, when you fix the bugs, could it be a very fun game! Please dont waste that Potential.

reviews.review_description: First of all, game is so addictive, every moments are memorable experience. Enemies looks creepy, and remind me of zombies from Resident Evil series.(Especially Plaga & Cadou) I liked variety of enemies, infected dogs, slime kind, and also dismembered into 2 part. Musics are very horribly atmospheric. Only thing I disliked is, game is quite short. It could be awesome with 1 or 2 chapters more.

Its honestly a good game i will complain about not being able to find much immortal in a few parts i also don't like how for the most of the game it seems like you might get to decided the ending just to have no choice, but still a fun game and i might play it again with a friend.

developer_response.body: We're constantly improving the game and hope you can have a chance to enjoy it more the second time!

reviews.review_description: I like it—the dark atmosphere, the beautiful graphics, the great music, and the high-quality voice acting. As I mentioned before, I really like it.

Dread Meridian terrified me—but not with those cheap jump scares, y’know? That Lovecraftian creepiness is done so incredibly right. The lighting details are insanely good; every single corner oozes real terror, and I catch myself wanting to glance over my shoulder every few steps.
The level of detail when you lean in to check things out in VR is insane. Instead of going for cheap jump scares, it builds this creepy vibe through atmosphere and story. That said, if you look closely, the lip-syncing’s a bit off, and some of the smaller props feel kinda rough around the edges—though let’s be real, I wasn’t around in the 1930s, so maybe that’s just how props actually looked back then! Still, the terrifying atmosphere more than makes up for these flaws.
Admittedly, the interaction mechanics are kinda “hardcore” every now and then—some interactions even require fairly big movements to trigger a response—but I got the hang of it super quick and got way better. Runs butter-smooth on my Q3—it’s such a blast! I haven’t finished the later parts yet, but I’m still more than happy to keep playing. If you’re into this kind of horror, you definitely have to give it a shot!!!

Games genuinely horrendous I’m 30 minutes in and can’t shoot the gun considering I’ve reloaded it 3 times and the healing mechanic does not work at all 

Sad as it looks interesting

reviews.review_description: Game is great if I could load my gun? How do you load it? Thanks please fix the adjusting your holster setting. Everytime you adjust it to your personal setting and you die it goes back to default

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! You can reload the gun by first unloading the clip with "B", then grabbing a clip from your chest area, loading the clip into the gun,  then finally, sliding the rack.

reviews.review_description: The game has potential, and the team is constantly updating it. I don't understand why you're being so strict. I hope you can give the team more opportunities.
The foundation of this game has a lot of potential, and the team is continuously fine-tuning it. It's genuinely scary. As mentioned in previous comments, we should support teams like this.

The trailers leading up to this game launch seemed very promising I was very excited and now that I have it and played it on the quest 3 the game absolutely sucks don't waste your money I don't know what happened or what the devs did but I'm in the beginning of the game where you first get the knife and you have to break a lock off a gate and I can't even break the lock off . the game is just broken and sucks I'm highly disappointed I would not recommend even thinking about this game until there is a massive patch from the devs

reviews.review_description:
It’s not perfect, has some bugs and some things are not fully polished, but I had a great experience overall. It gave me some RE vibes. The story is good, but the ending felt a bit too abrupt?? It’s hard to find this kind of game in VR, and I really appreciate that.

Some people mentioned they ran out of ammo, but I personally had plenty throughout the game.
Cool game but things are slow and jittery, especially the bayonet.
